sourceMovie.title: El Infierno
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto El Infierno (2010) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Once Upon a Time in Mexico
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If El Infierno clicked for you, Once Upon a Time in Mexico (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Shaft
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If El Infierno clicked for you, Shaft (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after El Infierno?
faq.answer: Try Once Upon a Time in Mexico, Shaft, Smokin' Aces—each shares DNA with El Infierno while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is El Infierno?
faq.answer: El Infierno (2010) is best described as Action, Crime, Western, Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is El Infierno worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
